description: A low-code conversational AI platform used to build custom agents, integrate with Power Platform, and connect to internal knowledge sources or APIs.
examClues: Look for phrases like "conversational AI", "custom agents", "low-code AI", or "connect to knowledge sources". If the question asks for a platform to build a conversational agent with custom topics, it's Copilot Studio.

description: Provides secure, enterprise-grade access to OpenAI’s language models (like GPT-4). Used for content generation, sentiment analysis, and answering questions from custom data.
examClues: Keywords: "enterprise-grade security", "GPT-4", "sentiment analysis", "custom language model". Choose this when needing secure API access to generative AI.

description: An AI-powered productivity tool integrated into Microsoft 365 apps (Word, Outlook, Teams) to automate tasks like summarizing emails and generating meeting notes.
examClues: Keywords: "summarize email threads", "meeting preparation", "Word", "Outlook", "Teams". If the goal is to enhance employee productivity within Office apps, this is the answer.

description: A suite of low-code tools (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI) used for building business applications, automated workflows, and data integrations.
examClues: Keywords: "low-code business apps", "workflow automation", "Power Apps". Often the correct choice when integrating multiple Microsoft business solutions.

description: A secure, scalable cloud database used by Power Platform and Dynamics 365 to store and manage business data, often serving as the primary knowledge base for agents.
examClues: Keywords: "Dynamics 365 data", "centralized business database", "Power Platform storage". This is the answer for storing app data securely within the Microsoft ecosystem.

description: A control plane for managing, evaluating, and deploying AI models and agents. Includes features for drift monitoring, responsible AI, and prompt evaluations.
examClues: Keywords: "drift monitoring", "evaluate model output", "prompt evaluations", "responsible AI dashboard". Choose this for model governance, testing, and lifecycle management.

description: Used to collect, analyze, and act on telemetry data from cloud environments and AI agents. Application Insights specifically tracks agent telemetry in near-real-time.
examClues: Keywords: "comprehensive telemetry data", "near-real-time (NRT)", "performance insights". Always pick Application Insights for monitoring custom apps and agents.

description: A unified data governance and risk management service. Used to monitor AI apps for compliance, track sensitive data, and apply data loss prevention (DLP).
examClues: Keywords: "data governance", "compliance", "track sensitive data", "DLP policies". This is the go-to answer for data security and compliance requirements.

description: Security policies configured to prevent sensitive data from being shared inappropriately or accessed by unauthorized connectors in Power Platform.
examClues: Keywords: "prevent agents from accessing sensitive data", "restrict connector usage". Select DLP when asked how to block data leakage in Power Platform.

description: An AI assistant designed for security professionals to quickly analyze threat signals, respond to incidents, and assess security posture.
examClues: Keywords: "security professionals", "threat signals", "incident response". Only choose this when the persona is a security analyst.

description: An AI pair programmer that assists developers by suggesting code and functions directly within their IDE.
examClues: Keywords: "write code faster", "developer IDE", "code suggestions". This is the answer for software development productivity.

description: The process of managing an application's lifecycle (development, testing, deployment). In Power Platform, this relies on Solutions (Managed/Unmanaged).
examClues: Keywords: "deploy between environments", "version control", "managed solutions". Pick solutions or pipelines when asked about moving agents to production.

description: A technique that grounds an AI model on external, domain-specific knowledge (like documents or databases) to improve accuracy and reduce hallucinations.
examClues: Keywords: "grounding data", "external knowledge", "reduce hallucinations". Look for RAG when asked how to make an AI answer based on company documents.

description: Unmanaged solutions are used in development environments and can be modified. Managed solutions are deployed to test/production and cannot be edited.
examClues: Keywords: "prevent direct editing in production", "deploy consistently". Always choose exporting as a Managed Solution when moving to Production.

description: A service used to create automated workflows (flows) between apps and services to synchronize files, collect data, and orchestrate backend processes.
examClues: Keywords: "automate a backend process", "workflow", "orchestrate data movement". Use this for step-by-step logic that runs independently of agent reasoning.

description: A portfolio of intelligent business applications (CRM and ERP) such as Sales, Customer Service, and Finance, which can be extended and automated using AI agents.
examClues: Keywords: "CRM", "ERP", "Sales", "Customer Service".

description: A tool that provides a single pane of glass to evaluate models for fairness, bias, error analysis, and interpretability.
examClues: Keywords: "fairness", "bias", "interpretability". Choose this when executives (like CEO) want to verify compliance with AI principles.

description: The foundational instructions given to an AI model or agent that define its persona, constraints, and behavior. Modifying these is the best way to mitigate biased behavior.
examClues: Keywords: "mitigate bias", "persona", "constraints". Modifying system instructions is the correct strategy for addressing bias without retraining the model.

description: Wrappers around a REST API that allow Power Platform and Copilot Studio to communicate with external services and data sources securely.
examClues: Keywords: "REST API", "external services", "custom integration". Use custom connectors when Copilot Studio needs to talk to a 3rd party API.

description: A tool in Azure Monitor used to edit and run log queries against collected telemetry data, often used for troubleshooting Copilot Studio agents.
examClues: Keywords: "log queries", "troubleshooting agents", "telemetry". Choose this when needing to dig deep into logs for comprehensive insights.

description: A constraint on the number of tokens an AI model can process. Chunking documents, reducing knowledge sources, and prompt refinement help manage this.
examClues: Keywords: "response time is slow", "token usage limit". Reconfiguring prompts to limit retrieved content is the best way to handle this.

description: An open standard that enables agents to dynamically use external tools at runtime without needing manual updates to the agent's internal logic.
examClues: Keywords: "dynamically use external tools at runtime", "without updating the agent". Look for MCP for dynamic tool use.

description: A metric or structured analysis evaluating the financial and strategic impact of AI initiatives, balancing immediate results with long-term values (e.g., using a horizon-based framework).
examClues: Keywords: "evaluate the financial implications", "balance immediate results with long-term values". Look for "horizon-based framework" or "TCO Calculator".

description: A tool used to evaluate the potential overall cost of an AI solution (including development, deployment, and operating costs) to support ROAI analysis.
examClues: Keywords: "potential cost of the solution", "ROAI analysis", "expected savings". TCO Calculator is the right answer for evaluating costs.

description: A feature allowing an agent to simulate user interactions across third-party apps and websites, such as clicking buttons or entering text.
examClues: Keywords: "simulating user interactions", "frequent changes to the app's user interface", "clicking buttons".
